title: Automated my TradingView strategy to Binance (no 3rd-party tools) — here’s what I learned
author: Economy_Fun5953
selftext:
When I posted about automating my TradingView strategy with Binance, almost everyone wanted $200+ just to fix the bot.  
I even tried using AI to solve the repeat-signal issue — no luck.

So I rolled up my sleeves and fixed it myself. Now it runs perfectly from TradingView → Binance with webhook alerts only. No 3rd-party apps (those just add cost).

The funny part? I uploaded my working strategy twice on TradingView as an invite-only script, even offered it for *free for a month*, and still got banned twice.  
Their reason? “Insufficient description.” Really? I literally explained everything clearly.

Anyway, I’m not here to sell anything — I just want to help other traders automate smarter and save money.  
Anyone else run into weird TradingView moderation issues like this?

replies.author: Tall_Instance9797
replies.body: A webhook is an automated way for one application to send real-time information to another application whenever a specific event occurs. For example if you create a telegram bot it will have a webhook (a URL) that listens for updates from another application, like hummingbot, which might send your TG bot information when an event occurs like creating a market order or filling a market order.

comments.author: Tall_Instance9797
comments.body: Well done. How long did it take you to both try and fix it with AI and roll up your sleeves and figure it out for yourself?

replies.author: Economy_Fun5953
replies.body: Ohh btw i didn’t took help of ai, i just understand where shall i focus n edit and i did it manually within 48hours

replies.author: Tall_Instance9797
replies.body: So you would rather spend 48 hours of your time over spending $200. That tells me everything I need to know. Thanks.

comments.author: AttitudeGrouchy33
comments.body:
This is the right approach. Rolling up your sleeves and fixing it yourself taught you way more than paying someone $200+ would have. Now you actually understand how it works and can troubleshoot when something breaks.



The repeat-signal issue is super common with webhook automation - most people hit that wall. The fact that you solved it yourself means you built real knowledge, not just bought a black box solution.



TradingView moderation is notoriously strict about scripts, even educational ones. They're paranoid about anything that looks like selling signals. "Insufficient description" is their catch-all when they're unsure. Frustrating but you found the workaround.



Sounds like you've got a solid setup now. The webhook-only approach keeps it simple and eliminates the middleman costs. I went through similar automation learning curve - the DIY struggle is where you actually learn what works: https://app.andmilo.com/?code=@milo4reddit

replies.author: Economy_Fun5953
replies.body: Thanks bud. Indeed! I wanted to built a system on my own. It took 7 years to find a good 3 strategy and now when i can fix my own code by myself without the knowledge of coding language. I believe that i have indeed created a system for me. Anyway thanks bro!

replies.author: AttitudeGrouchy33
replies.body: Good luck my friend!! seems like you are crushing it

replies.body: Alhamdulliah bro, just living my life need to achieve more. But anyway thanks mann!

comments.author: NegativeControl7507
comments.body:
Man, this is super interesting. I’ve been trying to automate my own strategies as well, trying to automate some stuff with N8N but for the moment I've only build this automation that just an extra confirmation layer for my trades. :  
  
 I send a pair → it pulls sentiment data (Fear & Greed + news), fetches prices, generates a TradingView-style chart, and my AI agent returns a final verdict: **Buy / Sell / Wait**, confidence score, and a short explanation.

I’d actually love to see how you structured your webhook setup and handled the signal logic.

If you’re open to sharing or explaining a bit more, I’m definitely interested.

replies.author: Economy_Fun5953
replies.body:
Hey nice mann! But honestly i tried all those paths like automating my trades based on ai decision back then 2 years ago, and it was all failure.

Finally i just put my proper trading strategy on pine script code and automate it directly on binance with webhook. 

Also, i srsly say if you looking to learn out here or on yt or anywhere some people will charge u money and some other will just say bullshit or tey to sell 3rd party, so try to automate it directly to binance or bybit. It will help i save tonnes of money.

replies.author: NegativeControl7507
replies.body:
Thanks for the feedback  
I’m really passionate about trading and I genuinely think it can still be linked with automation (if its done in a good way) and that’s exactly why I’m trying to dive deeper into how people like you approach it, especially those who’ve been in the space for a while and learned things that way.

I created a small Discord for builders and automation-nerds like us. My mate and I are working on a project around smarter, leaner crypto automation, but what we value most right now is real feedback from people with actual experience, not the usual “pay me for a course” crowd.  
  
If you’d ever be down to chat or share more of your experience, I’d love to have you there.

[https://discord.com/invite/c2QYkWZ3vw](https://discord.com/invite/c2QYkWZ3vw)

comments.author: Interesting_Kiwi_417
comments.body:
The time spent in learning is equivalent to the knowledge others have spent to learn. You either give the funds to someone and let them spend the time to build it or put your own time , its the same thing. 

My idea to anyone who is looking for some work or automation to be done in trading is that if they can't spend $200 to get something done then definately they dont find the other person time worth for it.

There are always three currencies in life: time, money and knowledge.  I hope you have just traded the currency of time on yourself instead of money which you will spend on someone else, and the end result is the third currency knowledge which you want to now sell at a cost , the invite only script so I guess everything fits into the same cycle.

replies.author: Economy_Fun5953
replies.body:
Totally agree — time, money, and knowledge are all currencies, and everyone chooses which one to spend.

In my case, it wasn’t about undervaluing someone’s time or avoiding paying — it was more about wanting to understand the problem myself, because if a bot fails later, I should know how to fix it instead of being dependent again.

I also didn’t try to *sell* anything — I actually offered it for free for a month and wanted to collaborate, but TradingView’s moderation blocked it. And bro i just wanted to help people with my strategy, i DONT charge anything penny for my trading, I just dont want to share my code instead i can just give them invite to use my straetgy and after a month if they think they are in profit then we split some percentage thats it.  
   
So yes, we all trade these currencies — I just chose to spend time so I could gain knowledge, and I still prefer sharing it rather than monetizing it. That’s what builds stronger traders and a better community overall.

replies.author: Economy_Fun5953
replies.body: Yes mannn.... I dunno i srsly wanna help ppl. Because you never know whose prayer gonna accepted. So bro, lets make our trading community folks build stronger and stronger forever.

comments.author: freshmuse
comments.body:
Yeah, that story sounds very familiar... getting Tradingview and Binance working correctly is way harder than people expect, and most of the pain isn’t the logic, it’s edge cases, duplicate signals, and reliability. Also agree on the no third-party instinct until you realize you’ve basically rebuilt one 😅and also  once you push past basic indicators, you start hitting opaque rules and random enforcement.

That’s honestly why a lot of traders eventually stop wiring things themselves and use an execution layer like Coinrule: same Tradingview style logic, but without fighting webhooks, bans, or maintenance every time something changes. Respect for actually vibecoding and shipping a working system but i'm curious how it behaves once market conditions really shift.

comments.author: deadline_dork23
comments.body: pickmytrade wouldve handled the tradingview to binance automation for you without any of that headache. You gotta work smart not hard.

replies.author: Economy_Fun5953
replies.body:
Well, i need to work hard because i want free solution and the thing u mentioned is charging 50 bucks per month and on binance i am doing it for free. 
Anyway thanks!
